mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value < 0.05, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in opposite direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing equity markets to price in lower future earnings and produce negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of periods where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or rolling IC is consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction investment, which directly reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with out-of-sample accuracy below 50% or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 205 on the CPIAUCSL index, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening and margin compression, which forces immediate equity repricing and generates negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns occur in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or the signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL exceeds 205.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and higher corporate output, which translates into improved earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during industrial production expansions, with Rank IC consistently below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 150 thousand signal robust labor demand and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when payrolls exceed 150 thousand, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal income confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined growth signal produces negative forward returns in > 55% of cases, or interaction term IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 105, manufacturing capacity utilization drops sharply, signaling an imminent economic contraction that forces equity markets to discount future earnings and produce negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally during industrial production declines below 105, with positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances.
